analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-345

POL-345.title: IMPLEMENT: Add Polaris function-level repository graph backend
POL-345.body:
## Objective

Implement a Polaris-native, function-level repository graph backend covering data model, parser, resolver, query API, and CLI surface.

## Context

Analysis from [POL-344](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-344>) (Analyze and restructure CodeGraph implementation cluster). The governance baseline was established in [POL-340](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-340>) (graph config, `.polaris/graph/` exclusion, `src/graph/governance.ts`). Prior orphaned issues [POL-337](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-337>) and [POL-338](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-338>) have been superseded by this cluster — they lacked a shared parent, canonical template compliance, correct module paths, and complete child coverage.

This cluster builds the minimum viable graph backend: SQLite store → tree-sitter extractor → resolver → query API → CLI.

## Goal

Deliver a working function-level repository graph that can be built on demand and queried for symbol lookups, call relationships, and impact analysis. Outputs live under `graph.outputPath` (`.polaris/graph/`). Graph data is advisory — not authoritative completion evidence — in phase 1.

## Scope

* src/graph/store/\*\*
* src/graph/parser/\*\*
* src/graph/resolver/\*\*
* src/graph/query/\*\*
* src/cli/graph.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Graph store module (SQLite schema, versioning, DB adapter, baseline queries) is implemented and tested
- [ ] Tree-sitter extraction pipeline runs deterministically over TypeScript/JavaScript and persists nodes/edges to graph store
- [ ] Resolver builds import and call-graph relationships in the graph store
- [ ] Query API exposes symbol lookup, callers/callees, impacted files, and graph statistics
- [ ] CLI surface provides `npm run polaris -- graph build`, `graph query`, and `graph impact`
- [ ] All graph artifacts written to `graph.outputPath`; MIT attribution preserved in NOTICES
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/graph/

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-340](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-340>) (graph artifact governance) — Done.

## Non-goals

* No worker packet / librarian integration in this cluster (deferred to follow-on cluster)
* No watcher or incremental rebuild in phase 1
* No wholesale CodeGraph adoption — Polaris-native implementation inspired by CodeGraph only
* Do not treat graph data as authoritative dispatch completion evidence in phase 1

POL-340.title: Implement graph artifact governance and lifecycle controls in Polaris runtime

POL-344.title: Analyze and restructure CodeGraph implementation cluster

POL-337.title: Implement CodeGraph data model and SQLite baseline in Polaris

POL-338.title: Implement tree-sitter extraction pipeline integration for Polaris code-intel

POL-350.title: Graph CLI surface — build / query / impact commands
POL-350.body:
## Objective

Add CLI commands for building, querying, and computing impact from the repository graph.

## Context

Fifth and final child of [POL-345](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-345>) (IMPLEMENT: Add Polaris function-level repository graph backend). Depends on [POL-349](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-349>) (query API) being Done. Exposes the graph backend through the Polaris CLI so operators can manually build the graph and inspect its output.

## Goal

Three CLI commands under `npm run polaris -- graph`:

1. `build` — runs parser pipeline + resolver over configured source roots; writes graph artifacts to `graph.outputPath`
2. `query <symbol>` — looks up a symbol and prints callers/callees as JSON (`--json`) or table
3. `impact <symbol>` — prints transitively impacted files for a symbol as JSON or table

## Scope

* src/cli/graph.ts
* src/cli/index.ts (register graph subcommand)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `npm run polaris -- graph build` runs parser + resolver, writes graph artifacts to `graph.outputPath`
- [ ] `npm run polaris -- graph query <symbol>` returns symbol lookup result as JSON (`--json`) or formatted table
- [ ] `npm run polaris -- graph impact <symbol>` returns impacted files as JSON or formatted table
- [ ] All three commands support `--dry-run` (print plan, do not write)
- [ ] Errors from graph build are surfaced as non-zero exit with message
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npm run polaris -- graph build --dry-run

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-349](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-349>) (query API) being Done.

## Non-goals

* No watcher or incremental rebuild commands
* No worker packet or dispatch integration (deferred to follow-on cluster)
* No graph visualization

POL-349.title: Graph query API — symbol lookup and impact queries
POL-349.body:
## Objective

Implement the graph query API: stable query functions for symbol lookup, callers/callees, impacted files, and graph statistics.

## Context

Fourth child of [POL-345](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-345>) (IMPLEMENT: Add Polaris function-level repository graph backend). Depends on [POL-346](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-346>) (graph store) and [POL-348](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-348>) (resolver) being Done. The query API is the stable interface through which CLI commands and future runtime callers access the graph. All response shapes must be stable and versioned.

## Goal

New module `src/graph/query/` with query functions:

1. `lookupSymbol(name, file?)` → `GraphSymbol | null`
2. `getCallers(symbolId)` → `GraphSymbol[]`
3. `getCallees(symbolId)` → `GraphSymbol[]`
4. `getImpactedFiles(symbolId)` → `GraphFile[]`
5. `getGraphStats()` → `{ nodeCount, edgeCount, fileCount, symbolCount }`

## Scope

* src/graph/query/index.ts
* src/graph/query/types.ts
* src/graph/query/index.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All five query functions implemented and exported from `src/graph/query/index.ts`
- [ ] Response types defined in `src/graph/query/types.ts` (no internal implementation fields exposed)
- [ ] `lookupSymbol` returns `null` for unknown symbols (never throws)
- [ ] `getImpactedFiles` traverses `CALLS` and `IMPORTS` edges transitively (depth-bounded to prevent cycles)
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/graph/

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-346](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-346>) (graph store) and [POL-348](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-348>) (resolver) being Done.

## Non-goals

* No CLI surface (Child 05)
* No worker packet integration (deferred to follow-on cluster)
* No streaming or subscription query patterns

POL-348.title: Graph resolver — import resolution and relationship builder
POL-348.body:
## Objective

Implement the graph resolver: resolve import paths, create caller/callee and file-ownership edges in the graph store.

## Context

Third child of [POL-345](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-345>) (IMPLEMENT: Add Polaris function-level repository graph backend). Depends on [POL-346](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-346>) (graph store) and [POL-347](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-347>) (parser/extraction) being Done. The extractor populates raw symbol nodes; the resolver builds the relationship edges that make the graph queryable for impact analysis.

## Goal

New module `src/graph/resolver/` with:

1. Import resolver: map import specifiers to graph file/symbol IDs using the extracted node table
2. Edge builder: create `CALLS`, `IMPORTS`, `DEFINED_IN` edges between resolved symbols and files
3. Unresolved symbol handling: record stubs with an `UNRESOLVED` flag rather than failing
4. Deterministic rebuild: resolver is idempotent — re-running clears and rebuilds edges

## Scope

* src/graph/resolver/index.ts
* src/graph/resolver/resolve-imports.ts
* src/graph/resolver/build-edges.ts
* src/graph/resolver/index.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Import specifiers resolved to graph symbol IDs where possible; marked `UNRESOLVED` otherwise
- [ ] `CALLS` edges created between caller and callee `GraphSymbol` nodes
- [ ] `IMPORTS` edges created between `GraphFile` nodes
- [ ] `DEFINED_IN` edges link symbols to their file
- [ ] Resolver is idempotent: re-running drops and rebuilds all edges without duplication
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/graph/

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-346](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-346>) (graph store) and [POL-347](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-347>) (parser) being Done.

## Non-goals

* No query API (Child 04)
* No cross-repo resolution
* No type-level relationship resolution

POL-347.title: Graph parser — tree-sitter extraction pipeline
POL-347.body:
## Objective

Implement the tree-sitter extraction pipeline: load grammars, run deterministic AST symbol extraction over TypeScript/JavaScript source files, and persist extracted nodes/edges into the graph store.

## Context

Second child of [POL-345](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-345>) (IMPLEMENT: Add Polaris function-level repository graph backend). Depends on [POL-346](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-346>) (graph store) being Done. [POL-338](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-338>) covered equivalent scope but targeted `src/cognition/code-intel/extraction/` (wrong module — extraction is a graph subsystem concern, not a cognition concern); this child supersedes it with correct placement under `src/graph/parser/`.

## Goal

New module `src/graph/parser/` with:

1. Grammar/runtime loader: load tree-sitter + language grammars with pinned versions
2. Extractor: walk AST, extract function/class/method/import declarations as `GraphSymbol`/`GraphNode` entries
3. Pipeline: accept a list of file paths, extract, persist into graph store via adapter
4. Error handling via Polaris runtime conventions (non-fatal per-file errors, summary at end)

## Scope

* src/graph/parser/loader.ts
* src/graph/parser/extract.ts
* src/graph/parser/pipeline.ts
* src/graph/parser/extract.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tree-sitter and language grammar versions pinned in `package.json`
- [ ] Extractor identifies function, class, method, and import declarations in TypeScript/JavaScript files
- [ ] Extraction results persisted to graph store via `src/graph/store/adapter`
- [ ] Extraction is deterministic — same input always produces same graph entries
- [ ] Per-file errors caught and surfaced as warnings without aborting the pipeline
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/graph/

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-346](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-346>) (graph store) being Done.

## Non-goals

* No import resolution or edge creation (Child 03)
* No watcher or incremental rebuild
* No language support beyond TypeScript/JavaScript in phase 1

POL-346.title: Graph store — data model and SQLite baseline
POL-346.body:
## Objective

Implement the graph store module: type system, SQLite schema, versioning, DB adapter boundary, and baseline query primitives.

## Context

First child of [POL-345](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-345>) (IMPLEMENT: Add Polaris function-level repository graph backend). The graph governance framework (`src/graph/governance.ts`, `GraphConfig`) was established in [POL-340](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-340>). [POL-337](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-337>) covered equivalent scope but targeted `src/map/codegraph/` (wrong module) and lacked canonical template compliance; this child supersedes it with correct placement under `src/graph/store/`.

## Goal

New module `src/graph/store/` with:

1. Type system: node/edge/file/symbol taxonomy (`GraphNode`, `GraphEdge`, `GraphFile`, `GraphSymbol`, etc.)
2. SQLite schema baseline with schema versioning table
3. DB adapter: open/close, schema init, migration guard
4. Baseline query primitives: insertNode, insertEdge, lookupSymbol, lookupFile

## Scope

* src/graph/store/types.ts
* src/graph/store/schema.sql
* src/graph/store/adapter.ts
* src/graph/store/queries.ts
* src/graph/store/adapter.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `GraphNode`, `GraphEdge`, `GraphFile`, `GraphSymbol` types defined and exported from `src/graph/store/types.ts`
- [ ] SQLite schema includes `nodes`, `edges`, `files`, `symbols` tables with `schema_version` tracking
- [ ] DB adapter opens and initializes the schema; is idempotent on repeat calls
- [ ] `insertNode`, `insertEdge`, `lookupSymbol`, `lookupFile` implemented in `queries.ts`
- [ ] MIT attribution recorded in governance NOTICES on adapter init (via `writeGraphNotices` from `src/graph/governance.ts`)
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/graph/

## Ordering

No dependencies — executes first in cluster.

## Non-goals

* No tree-sitter extraction (Child 02)
* No relationship resolution (Child 03)
* No query API (Child 04)
* No CLI surface (Child 05)
